title: Parenting Skills Classes * Fathers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Parenting Skills Classes * Fathers 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community At Risk Families Child Abuse Issues Disabilities Issues Divorce/Custody/Support Issues Domestic Violence Issues Fathers Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorders Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Native American Community Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Sexual Assault Issues Substance Use Disorder Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details PATHFINDER NETWORK PATHFINDER NETWORK CENTER FOR FAMILY SUCCESS BUILDING RESILIENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Skill-based support group for men who have experienced adversity, stress and trauma. Meals are provided and childcare is available. 7305 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Fathers involved with child welfare and/or the criminal justice system who have some form of contact with their children. This can include supervised visitation or regular phone contact. Hours: Classes are generally held weekly in the evenings Email (503) 892-5396 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SQUIRES KNIGHT PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a course for individuals interested in expanding the bounds of fatherhood. Program participants will complete 50 hours of reading to their children, 2 hours of financial literacy education, 2 hours of parenting education, and other courses over a 6 month period. Upon completion of the program, participants receive a family photo package, t-shirt, and a $100 gift certificate. Free pizza is offered at all meetings. Eligibility: Males age 14-25 who are parents or parents to be Hours: September-June: 3rd Thursday of the month starting at 6:30pm. Summer hours vary; check Facebook for location information. Email (503) 421-0933 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PATHFINDER NETWORK BUILDING RESILIENCE
